question_text: A student wants to make an electromagnet that can pick up 50 paper clips. She tests three designs: Design 1 has 100 coils with 3 amps of current, Design 2 has 200 coils with 1.5 amps of current, and Design 3 has 150 coils with 2 amps of current. Which design will produce the strongest electromagnet?

options.label: A
options.text: Design 1 because it has the highest current

options.label: B
options.text: Design 2 because it has the most coils

options.label: C
options.text: Design 3 because it balances coils and current equally

options.label: D
options.text: All designs will be equally strong

correct_answer: B) Design 2 because it has the most coils
explanation: Electromagnet strength depends on both the number of coils and the current, but the number of coils is generally more important. Design 2 has 200 coils (the most) and even though the current is lower, the extra coils more than compensate, making it the strongest overall.
